findings.summary: Hook registered with relative path — fails silently if CWD differs from project root
findings.detail: settings.json registers the hook as `python3 ".claude/skills/mcp-sentinel/hooks/sentinel_preflight.py"`. If Claude Code is invoked from a directory other than the project root, Python will not resolve this relative path and the hook will fail-open (allow all). No security gap in normal usage; protection silently disappears if the CWD assumption breaks.
findings.recommendation: Consider using an absolute path in settings.json if portability is needed.

findings.detail: New known-malicious domains or dangerous patterns discovered since April 2026 are not covered. The bundled iocs.json relies on manual updates from the project maintainer.
findings.recommendation: Re-run Sentinel periodically and check for updates to claude-mcp-sentinel.

inventory.name: session-start-hook
inventory.source: built-in (Anthropic / Claude Code on the web)

mcp_servers.name: supermemory-mcp

findings.summary: Memories are stored on Supermemory's cloud servers (api.supermemory.ai)
findings.detail: This is by design — cross-session persistence requires cloud storage. Any content you ask the AI to remember is transmitted to and stored on Supermemory's infrastructure. Do not store highly sensitive secrets via this tool.
findings.recommendation: Be mindful of what data you ask the AI to remember. Self-hosting is possible if you supply your own SUPERMEMORY_API_KEY.

findings.detail: The server includes PostHog integration (controlled via POSTHOG_API_KEY env var server-side). Usage analytics may be sent to PostHog from the server side.
findings.recommendation: No action needed from client side. This is a server-side configuration by supermemory.ai.

findings.summary: whoAmI tool discloses user identity to the AI
findings.detail: The whoAmI tool returns user ID, email, name, client, and session info — data obtained during OAuth authentication. This is used for memory personalization.
findings.recommendation: Expected behavior for a personalized memory service.

coherence_verdict.stated_purpose: Persistent AI memory across LLM sessions via cloud storage and OAuth authentication
consistent_actions: recall tool — retrieves stored memories via semantic search
whoAmI tool — identifies user for memory personalization
OAuth auth against api.supermemory.ai — required for per-user memory isolation
Cloud storage on api.supermemory.ai — required for cross-session persistence
suspicious_actions: PostHog telemetry (server-side) — not harmful but adds a third-party data recipient

mcp_servers.verdict: CLEAN — safe to install with privacy awareness

community_alerts.summary: CVE-2025-59536 / CVE-2026-21852: Claude Code itself vulnerable to RCE and API key exfiltration via malicious project settings files (hooks + ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL override). Not specific to any skill — affects the Claude Code platform. Patched by Anthropic.

Attackers can inject malicious hooks via .claude/settings.json in a cloned repo, executing commands before the user confirms trust. Sentinel's runtime hook is itself registered in this file — underscoring the importance of only installing skills from trusted sources.
